components.description: Your next bullet deals bonus spirit damage .

sections.Notes: The item provides a passive effect that creates an additional bullet when the hero shoots their weapon, and the additional bullet will mimic the bullets created by the current weapon. The Mystic Shot bullet appears as a glowing purple version of the hero's regular bullets. For most weapons, the Mystic bullet can follow the trajectory of any bullet, which can make it fly off-center for weapons with significant spread over time, such as McGinnis '. The exception are spreadshot weapons such as Abrams ' and Shiv 's, in which the Mystic pellet is always fired at the center of the crosshair. For explosive alternate fire weapons such as Viscous ' and Yamato 's, the Mystic bullet will appear the same but cannot deal damage in an area. Instead, it only deals the Spirit damage to the closest enemy to impact. The Mystic bullet hits before regular bullets. So for example, Bullet Resist Shredder 's effect is applied first and amplifies the weapon damage, but Crippling Headshot 's Spirit Resist reduction is applied after and does not amplify Mystic Shot's spirit damage. The additional Mystic bullet cannot deal critical (headshot) damage and is not affect by Ricochet . A Mystic bullet is immune to evasion .

sections.Notes: With no other items, Alchemical Fire (with its default +8 Spirit Power) deals a total of 384 Spirit Damage. Alchemical Fire deals damage instantly on contact, and then every 0.5 seconds thereafter, for a total of 10 damage instances. With no duration extenders, Alchemical Fire finishes dealing damage at 4.5 seconds in (the final 0.5 seconds applies the weapon damage effect only). Alchemical Fire ramps up its damage gradually, starting at 50% of the max dps at the start, and dealing max dps (100%) from 3.0 seconds and onward. With duration extenders, Alchemical Fire will continue dealing damage in further 0.5 sec intervals, e.g. at 5.0 sec, 5.5 sec, 6.0 sec, etc. These damage instances will keep using the max dps values.

sections.Notes: Using Cultist Sacrifice on an NPC gives the player the full soul bounty plus 180% (270% total). The original bounty and the bonus are displayed as two separate values when activated. The buff persists through death. Cultist Sacrifice applies additional damage to Non-Player Characters. This includes Troopers , lane structures like Guardians , Walkers , neutral entities such as Denizens , and the Patron . It does apply additional damage against Graves ' Ghouls , but not against McGinnis ' Mini Turrets . However, both of them can be consumed for the buff and bonus souls. Cultist Sacrifice's active counts as Spirit Damage . This means it can activate on-hit spirit damage procs such as Spirit Burn . When Cultist Sacrifice comes off cooldown, a whispering voice can be heard demanding a sacrifice.

components.description: Your bullets build up a Bleed on enemies, causing them to lose a percentage of their Max Health over time. Also applies Healing Reduction on the bleeding target.

sections.Notes: Bleed damage can kill. The bleed effect is indicated by puddles of blood dropping from the player. Bleed damage applies or "ticks" every half-second, dealing slightly over 1.2% max health spirit damage per tick. This means that, by default, bleed applies damage six times every time it is successfully built up for 7.5% max health spirit damage. Bleed Damage is considered Spirit Damage and is reduced by Spirit Resist . The duration of the bleed is affected by the Ability Duration stat, such as from Superior Duration . The extra damage is applied proportionally. For instance, since Superior Duration adds .84 seconds to the bleed effect, the bleed will deal 2.1% extra spirit damage for a total of 9.6% max health spirit damage. Headshots do not affect the Build-Up per shot. Does not build-up with melee hits, unlike other weapon build-up items ( Slowing Bullets etc.) Bleed does not build on any structures, such as Guardians and Walkers . Mid-boss is also immune to bleed. Bleed does build on Troopers and Denizens , but deals 50% damage. The healing reduction only applies while the target is bleeding. The healing reduction stacks with other sources of healing reduction. For instance, Healbane and Toxic Bullets will apply a healing reduction effect of 55%. The buildup per shot is also affected by weapon falloff, requiring more shots for the bleed to occur at further distances. Once applied, the bleed damage itself is unaffected by distance. For heroes with spreadshot weapons ( Abrams , Calico , Yamato , etc.), the listed bleed buildup applies on a shot where every pellet hits its target. The buildup is divided evenly between each pellet and missing one pellet will proportionally decrease buildup. For characters with burst-fire weapons ( Lash , Seven , etc.), the listed bleed buildup applies for each bullet rather than each burst. Landing a full burst as Lash, for instance, will build 45.9% of bleed on a target. For characters with splash damage alternate fire weapons ( Visco
sections.Buildup Per Shot: The item has a different buildup per shot depending on which hero purchased the item. The bleed only triggers on an enemy once the buildup reaches 100%, and the buildup decays back to 0 over time if the enemy has not been shot recently. Subsequent shots on a bleeding enemy (bullets or melee) refreshes the duration of the bleed. components.description: Your next parry protects you from the damage and effects of enemy abilities and items . On a successful spell parry heal and gain move speed and Spirit .

sections.Notes: Counterspell is visually indicated by a purple bubble VFX replacing the default parry effect. Counterspell only goes on cooldown on a successful spell parry. The player can parry multiple abilities during the Counterspell parry window. The heal and buffs are only applied for the first parry.
sections.Notable interactions: Counterspell prevents all damage from a Walker stomp and Shrine defense, but does not prevent the stun and knockback. It also does not prevent the Shrine's Curse status effect. Counterspell prevents all damage and Curse effect from the Patron 's defensive attack. Counterspell does not proc when parrying Viscous ' Puddle Punch , since the ability can be blocked by regular parries. Counterspell can prevent Lash 's Death Slam if the player parries at the moment they would be pulled. Counterspell can be used on The Doorman 's Hotel Guest . Counterspell can be used on Celeste 's Shining Wonder . However, the orb will continue to linger on the player and can bounce onto other heroes. Counterspell can prevent Build-Up effects such as Mina 's Love Bites and stacking effects such as Haze 's Fixation , if activated either before the build-up is applied or when it triggers. If build-up/stacks have already been applied, it does not prevent the build-up itself. Counterspell can prevent Mirage 's Djinn's Mark before it's applied to the player. However, it does not prevent it from stacking or triggering once it has been applied. Counterspell can block instant abilities such as Mo & Krill 's Combo if the enemy tries to use the ability during the target's parry window, similarly to Unstoppable .

sections.Movement: The minimum angle that you can leap at is 30°. When leaping, it will launch you at a peak of 1063 u/s upwards and outwards. You can choose to descend after a second in any direction and any angle under 45°. During the duration of which you are airborne, you have 100% air control; this allows for better air strafing and turning. This state and the ability to quickly descend ends immediately upon riding a Zipline or landing. When landing, your velocity will be reduced to ~500 u/s. It is possible to cancel your descent and this slowdown by dashing or jumping. This is called Majestic Leap Cancelling .

sections.Movement: At any range, Rescue Beam will fully pull your target towards you in one second. The speed that Rescue Beam pulls targets is typically fastest at the start. The peak speed is also dependent on distance between the target and the caster. At the max range of 34 meters, the peak speed is ~1700 u/s. The target will decelerate in speed until next to the caster.
sections.Notes: If self-cast, the item holder cannot pull themselves, and only one of two heals will be received. Rescue Beam is a channel, and can be cancelled by Stun and Interrupt if used on the caster, ending the heal and preventing the use of the pull. Targets already being pulled will continue to be pulled. Silence does not interrupt Rescue Beam, as it has no effect on items or channels. Allies will be moved to the current location of the item holder over the duration of the pull, not where the pull was activated.

components.description: Gain a chance to either deflect incoming bullets, preventing all weapon damage or prevent all on-hit effects from bullets.

sections.Notes: The chance for Deflection and On-hit Prevention is rolled individually, although Deflection will prevent most effects that On-hit prevention does. They can both trigger at the same time. This is most evident when testing Headhunter against Plated Armor. If a bullet is deflected, it will fly off in a different direction, maintaining its damaging capability.

Burst weapons ( ): Applied per bullet. Celeste : When deflected, an extra projectile is created. The original projectile can ricochet, as normal, and a new projectile is shot out in a random direction. The new projectile also ricochets. components.description: Your bullets temporarily steal Max HP from enemies. Enemies regain their stolen health when the debuff expires.

sections.Notes: Shooting an enemy hero deals 2.5% max HP damage and applies the Siphon Bullets debuff, which reduces the target's max HP by the amount of damage dealt, and increases the user's max HP while healing them by the same amount. The max HP steal lasts for 17 seconds (affected by Debuff Resist ), refreshes on subsequent applications, and stacks infinitely. Stealing max HP from an enemy places the effect on a 1.2s cooldown (affected by Item Cooldown Reduction ). Each application will steal up to 2.5% max HP from afflicted targets, but the amount siphoned is reduced with falloff and the flat damage dealt may interact with certain passives. The damage dealt by Siphon Bullets cannot be increased or decreased by Bullet Resist, Spirit Resist, or damage amplification ( Pocket 's Barrage or Escalating Exposure ). Shiv 's Bloodletting passive will reduce the flat damage dealt by Siphon Bullets, but Shiv will still lose 2.5% max HP per application. The damage dealt by Siphon Bullets will still trigger Abrams ' Infernal Resilience regeneration passive. The damage dealt by Siphon Bullets does not proc item effects. Siphon Bullets does not trigger if the initial bullet deals 0 damage. Siphon Bullets can not be applied on multiple targets by Ricochet due to its 1.2s cooldown, only the initial target will receive the debuff. Due to the max HP steal being based on the current max HP, which decreases after each proc, the damage dealt decays exponentially, meaning subsequent applications deal less damage. Siphon Bullets' current and max HP increase for its user can proc Healing Tempo , but is not affected by healing reduction. For each instance of max HP steal on an enemy, the user gains the same amount of max HP, equal to the stolen health. When an enemy has their max HP steal duration refreshed, the whole amount of max HP that was gained from that enemy also has its duration refreshed. If max HP has been gained from multiple enemies, refreshing the steal duration on one enemy will only re
sections.Trivia: Before the March 6, 2026 update, Siphon Bullets could permanently steal Max HP on hero kill, with the killer losing the stolen health if they died. This allowed a player to get "negative health" and become unkillable by dying multiple times under the effect of Siphon Bullets.

changelog.change: Now steals 2.5% of enemy max HP per bullet instead of a fixed amount. (Undocumented) : No longer permanently steals max HP on enemy kill.
Cooldown increased from 0.8s to 1.2s.

components.description: Temporarily suppress negative status effects and become immune to Stun, Silence, Sleep, Root, and Disarm . Cannot be used while Stunned or Slept .

sections.Notes: Unstoppable is not usable when the player loses control of their character, such as when asleep or stunned. Instead, it should be used in anticipation of these abilities to prevent them from working. Status Effects such as Anti-heal and Damage Debuffs (such as those applied from Inhibitor and Silencer ) are NOT suppressed. Channelled abilities such as Dynamo 's Singularity will affect the hero if Unstoppable's active ends while the ability is still ongoing. Unstoppable's active ability does not prevent damage from enemies, even if it prevents the negative effects. Although not explicitly mentioned in its description, the usage of Unstoppable also grants the user immunity to the Interrupt status effect. Unstoppable is not removed by Cursed Relic and blocks all of it's effects, including the removal of non-ultimate buffs.

sections.Notes: Mystic Burst uses Charge-Up Heroes with Spirit Lifesteal will heal from Mystic Burst's passive effect triggering. A small lightning shock visual is applied to the enemy hero when it is hit by Mystic Burst's passive effect. When the target is hit with spirit damage , the damage value which is compared against the triggering threshold is the base damage value prior to any increases or decreases from damage amp, damage reduction, spirit resistance, and spirit resistance reduction. Damage amp and spirit resist reduction will not help you in achieving the damage threshold to trigger the passive. Damage reduction and the target's spirit resistance will not prevent the passive from triggering when it otherwise would. While the tooltip only specifies 'Abilities', only Spirit Damage will activate Mystic Burst. Abilities dealing weapon damage - such as Venator 's Consecrating Grenade or Viscous ' Puddle Punch - will not activate Mystic Burst. The additional damage, and the ability which triggered the additional damage, are applied to the target as separate instances of damage. The additional damage from Mystic Burst is dealt after the original spell damage.

components.description: After you Dash-Jump , the next ability you use within ArcaneSurgeWindows will have bonus Range, Duration, and Spirit Power .

sections.Notes: Items and passive abilities cannot benefit from the conditional buff - they will not gain additional spirit power, duration, or range. The buff which provides the player with the conditional ability range, ability duration, and spirit power lasts for 7 seconds. The first ability cast after gaining the buff will consume the buff. The conditional ability range and ability duration are snapshot on ability use. The conditional spirit power is tied to the 7 second timer of the conditional buff. The +20 spirit power will only be available for 7 seconds after the player initially gains the conditional buff. Examples: 6 seconds after gaining the conditional buff, Vindicta uses Assassinate . This consumes the buff, and applies +20 spirit power to Assassinate 's damage. 6 seconds after gaining the conditional buff, Seven uses Lightning Ball . The buff is consumed. The Lightning Ball gains +15% ability duration and +12% ability range. The Lightning Ball gains +20 spirit power for 1 second only. For the rest of its duration, it does not benefit from +20 spirit power. 6 seconds after gaining the conditional buff, Seven uses Power Surge . The buff is consumed. Power Surge gains +15% ability duration and +12% ability range. Power Surge 's damage benefits from +20 spirit power for 1 second only. For the rest of its duration, the damage does not benefit from +20 spirit power. Power Surge 's total duration scales natively with spirit power at time of cast. The additional duration due to the +20 spirit power is snapshot on cast, and is not lost when the 7s conditional buff is consumed.

sections.Notes: Tankbuster is a Charge-Up item, so it's not affected by Cooldown Reduction . Tankbuster only goes on cooldown when it affects enemy heroes. It will not go on cooldown when you hit an NPC . Some forms of Spirit Amp applied by items count as separate damage instances and thus don't contribute to the single hit such as Escalating Exposure . When the target is hit with spirit damage, the damage value which is compared against the triggering threshold is the base damage value prior to any increases or decreases from damage amp, damage reduction, spirit resistance, and spirit resistance reduction. Damage amp and spirit resist reduction will not help you in achieving the damage threshold to trigger the passive. Damage reduction and the target's spirit resistance will not prevent the passive from triggering when it otherwise would. Interaction with spirit shields: The triggering of the additional damage is entirely unaffected by the target's barriers (or lack thereof). The additional damage, and the ability which triggered the additional damage, are applied to the target as separate instances of damage. The additional damage from Tankbuster is dealt after the original spell damage.

components.description: Imbue an ability with Lifesteal and to apply slow on a hit target and deal an additional percentage of the damage dealt to enemies around the target after a short delay.

sections.Notes: The debuff effect is applied to the first enemy hit. If the imbued ability is an area-of-effect and it hits multiple heroes, only one enemy will receive the debuff.
sections.Notable Interactions: Pure Damage does not trigger the reverb or use the imbued lifesteal. If an ability has bonus Pure Damage like the blessed stakes of Venator 's Ira Domini , it will not be used to calculate the damage of the reverb. Mystic Reverb can be imbued on Lady Geist 's Soul Exchange , but it has no effect. Abilities that only deal Melee Damage such as Viscous ' Puddle Punch or Calico 's Leaping Slash can be imbued, though they cannot activate the reverb or use the imbued lifesteal. Spirit Snatch applies after the melee damage and does not allow melee abilities to use Mystic Reverb. However, Quicksilver Reload and Mercurial Magnum grant bonus spirit damage to the imbued ability's first hit of weapon or spirit damage and can activate the debuff. The debuff can store damage from items like Quicksilver Reload or Tankbuster on the imbued ability. The 50% extra damage is calculated based on pre-mitigation damage, but can then still be mitigated by spirit resistance when it triggers.
sections.Known Bugs: The initial reverb damage can kill the primary target, but is sometimes non-lethal to secondary targets, leaving them with at least 1 hp remaining. This seems to mainly happen against Target Dummies in Sandbox

sections.Notable Interactions: Indomitable will prevent the player from being teleported to The Baroness if hit with The Doorman 's Hotel Guest . Indomitable will not be triggered by Dynamo 's Singularity . Indomitable is currently not triggered by Apollo 's Itani Lo Sahn . Indomitable will trigger if the player is dragged down by Lash 's Ground Strike and Billy 's Bashdown , but will not detach the player from their immobilize & pull effects, which continue until they reach the floor. While performing a similar effect, Phantom Strike 's pull does not trigger Indomitable. Indomitable will prevent players from being stunned after being parried. Indomitable will prevent players from being stunned after getting hit by bullets while on a zipline. They will still fall off the zipline.
